Explanation: This report presents a systematic approach to characterizing trace impurities in chromatographic data for biopharmaceutical quality control. Strengths include: clear methodology with three well-defined phases, appropriate statistical techniques (ALS baseline correction, SNR filtering, OLS regression, IQR outlier detection), and logical progression. The writing is generally professional and the abstract effectively summarizes the work. However, several limitations reduce the overall score: (1) No actual results are shown—the Results section discusses what "would" be found rather than presenting real data, figures, or outcomes; (2) The promised heatmap visualization (Figure 1) is not included; (3) Critical experimental details lack specificity (e.g., actual sample sizes after filtering, regression R² values, p-values); (4) The ALS implementation in Code 1 appears mathematically questionable and may not correctly implement the standard algorithm; (5) The decision rule linking p-values to batch vs. process dependency oversimplifies causality. Originality is modest—the workflow applies established techniques without novel methodological contributions. The appendix includes only partial code snippets without complete implementation details.
